name: good time to schedule
say: I'm reaching out to confirm we received your inquiry, and wanted to schedule a time for you to meet with one of our CPAs to dive into your situation a bit further. Is this a good time to get your appointment scheduled?
instruction: If the user is hesitant: "I understand timing can be tricky. This will just take a couple minutes to get scheduled."

name: wants callback
say: But before I close out your inquiry, is there a better time I can call you back to get you scheduled with one of our CPAs?

fields.name: wants_callback
fields.description: Whether the user wants a callback at a better time ('yes'), doesn't want to schedule at all ('no'), or changed their mind and wants to proceed now ('do it now').

transitions.say: Perfect, let me get that scheduled for you.

name: website booking option
say: No problem, if you change your mind, you can always find some time on our website using the "Talk to a CPA" button on the top right. Thank you for your time and have a wonderful day.

name: collect details intro
say: First, let's make sure we have all your contact information.

name: confirm name
say: I have your name as {contact_full_name}, is that correct?

name: confirm email
say: And what's the best email address for you?
instruction: If we have their email on file as {contact_email}, confirm it: "I have your email as {contact_email}, is that correct?" If they correct it, capture the updated email.

fields.name: confirmed_email
fields.description: The user's confirmed or corrected email address.

name: confirm phone
say: And what's the best phone number to reach you?
instruction: If we have their phone on file as {contact_phone}, confirm it: "I have your phone number as {contact_phone}, is that correct?" If they correct it, capture the updated number.

fields.name: confirmed_phone
fields.description: The user's confirmed or corrected phone number.

name: proceed to scheduling
say: Before we schedule anything, I want to let you know that because we're so close to the April 15 deadline, our CPA availability for new client meetings is extremely limited. While there may be a few openings, it is most likely that a meeting would happen after April 15. Would that be okay?
instruction:
The user must explicitly agree before you look for slots.

If user says no: "I completely understand, and I'm sorry we can't support that timeline right now. Is there anything else we can help you with today?"
If user is not interested: "Ok, thank you for your time, bye!"
If asked why: "Our CPAs are fully focused on completing returns for current clients during tax season, so they have very limited capacity to meet with new clients before April 15. Would you still like to schedule a meeting?"
If asked about filing this year: "We generally do not file new client returns that come in after March 1 by April 15. More commonly, we file an extension."
If asked what an extension is: "An extension is an extension of time to file, but not necessarily an extension of time to pay. Typically, a tax payment still needs to be made by April 15, and the return can then usually be filed by October 15."

fields.name: proceed_to_scheduling
fields.description: Whether the user expressed explicit consent to continue and schedule a meeting. 'implicit' for implicit consent.

name: faqs and call end
say: Before I let you go, do you have any questions for me that I can help answer?
instruction:
Answer any questions the user has. After answering, ask if they have additional questions and wait for the answer.

When the user has no more questions or is ready to end the call:
- If a meeting was scheduled: "You're all set for your meeting on {%meeting_scheduled_time_local%:words}. Thank you and have a wonderful day!"
- If no meeting was scheduled: "Thank you and have a wonderful day!"

fields.name: user_has_more_questions
fields.description: Whether the user still has questions. Set to false when they confirm they have no more questions or are ready to end the call.
